method: multi-agent verify + adversarial falsification pass (fresh contexts); verifier used WebSearch/WebFetch across TechCrunch, CNBC, MacRumors, CourtListener, AppleInsider, CNN, Bloomberg, Axios, 9to5Mac; falsifier independently re-checked case identity via CourtListener, cross-fetched TechCrunch and an AP wire render (clickorlando.com) to confirm the OpenAI quote outside the verifier's source set
origin_items: data/raw/2026-07-10/techcrunch-ai.json — Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
data/raw/2026-07-10/the-verge-ai.json — Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets
data/raw/2026-07-10/cnbc-technology.json — Apple sues OpenAI alleging trade secret theft, says scheme was 'at every level'

claims.text: Apple filed suit against OpenAI, Tang Tan, Chang Liu, and io Products LLC in N.D. Cal. on 10 July 2026 (Apple Inc. v. Liu, No. 5:26-cv-07078), alleging trade secret misappropriation and breach of contract
claims.reasoning: Filing event confirmed via direct CourtListener docket match (case number, court, defendants Chang Liu / OpenAI Foundation / OpenAI Group PBC / Tang Yew Tan / io Products LLC) plus independent quality-press accounts (TechCrunch, CNBC, Bloomberg, CNN, Axios, AppleInsider, 9to5Mac, MacRumors) that were fetched or corroborated across two independent agent passes.

claims.text: Apple's complaint alleges Chang Liu kept an Apple laptop and downloaded confidential hardware files after leaving, and exploited a bug to access Apple cloud storage post-departure
claims.reasoning: Every account traces to Apple's complaint (one origin) regardless of how many outlets repeat it -- per the independence rule this is single-source and published strictly as an Apple allegation, never as established fact.

claims.text: Apple's complaint alleges Tang Tan used Apple project code names while recruiting and coached departing employees on evading Apple security
claims.reasoning: Traces to the complaint, one origin; attributed to Apple in the published text.

claims.text: Apple's complaint alleges misuse of a proprietary metal-finishing technique and confidential vendor/manufacturing-sourcing information
claims.reasoning: Only independently located in one outlet's detailed reporting (AppleInsider); not contradicted, but not independently reproduced by the falsifier either -- kept single-source and attributed.

claims.text: Apple sent OpenAI a warning letter in February 2026 before filing suit
claims.reasoning: Upgraded from single-source during adversarial review: independently corroborated by TechCrunch's own reporting and a separate AP wire account (via clickorlando.com), two genuinely separate reporting chains.

claims.text: OpenAI spokesperson Drew Pusateri said OpenAI has 'no interest in other companies' trade secrets' and is still reviewing the filing
claims.reasoning: Quote verified verbatim and consistent across independently-fetched sources (TechCrunch fetch, AP wire fetch via clickorlando.com); mandatory per policy to include the accused party's response.

notes: A Wired headline was mentioned in initial triage ('Apple Is Suing OpenAI for Allegedly Stealing Hardware Secrets') but neither the verifier nor the falsifier could locate a real Wired article covering this story after dedicated searches -- excluded from sources, not cited. CNBC, CourtListener's full page, and AppleInsider direct fetches returned 403 for both agents; case facts and quotes were instead corroborated via TechCrunch direct fetch, an independent AP wire render, and CourtListener's docket metadata surfaced in search results. All allegation-level claims are attributed to Apple's complaint throughout the article per the litigation policy (published at dispute stage, no court ruling yet).
